title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (April 1)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: A two-family property on Adamson St. in Allston sold for $1,200,000.
A condo in Boston's 682 Atlantic Ave. was sold for $14,131,000.
A one-family contemporary home in Andover sold for $2,000,000.

title: Drake Maye surprises fan wearing ‘I [Heart] Drake Maye’ t-shirt at Bruins game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Drake Maye and Ann Michael attended a Bruins game at TD Garden, receiving a warm welcome from fans.
A fan wearing an 'I [Heart] Drake Maye' shirt was highlighted on the videoboard, prompting Maye to salute them.
Earlier, Drake Maye participated in a charity event in Quincy, raising over $4 million for Boston Children's Hospital.

title: Former Celtics coach Doc Rivers reportedly voted into Basketball Hall of Fame
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Doc Rivers, former Celtics coach, will be inducted into the Basketball Hall of Fame in 2026.
Rivers led the Celtics to an NBA title in 2008, their 17th championship, during his nine-year tenure.
In 27 coaching seasons, Rivers has a record of 1,191-861 and coached the Celtics and other teams.

title: Drake Maye shaves his head as part of charity event benefiting Boston Children’s Hospital
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots QB Drake Maye shaved his head at Granite's 'Saving by Shaving' event benefiting Boston Children's Hospital, raising $4.15 million.
Granite Telecommunications donated $2,500 per head shaved or 8 inches of hair donated, matched by CEO Rob Hale's family.
Other participants included Joe Andruzzi, Greg Hill, Quincy Mayor Thomas Koch, and Boston Children's CEO Kevin Churchwell.

title: Boston police officer criminally charged after allegedly leaving loaded gun unsecured in vehicle
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston police officer Sean Cullen faces charges for allegedly leaving his loaded gun unsecured in his vehicle.
The charges followed a domestic dispute in December, and Cullen has pleaded not guilty and is on administrative leave.
Cullen's law enforcement certification is suspended, and he earned $195,000 last year, according to city payroll records.

title: N.H. governor’s security aid rescues Mass. driver from fiery toll booth crash
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: N.H. Gov. Kelly Ayotte and a trooper rescued a Mass. driver from a fiery crash at Bedford Toll Plaza.
The trooper assigned to Ayotte's security detail pulled the trapped driver from the flaming vehicle, saving their life.
The seriously injured driver was hospitalized; no charges filed yet, and the crash is under investigation.

title: Mike Vrabel talks A.J. Brown, Patriots draft, and other takeaways from NFL owners’ meetings
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots coach Mike Vrabel discussed the team's ongoing interest in trading for Eagles' star A.J. Brown.
Vrabel emphasized seeking 'best player available' over drafting for need at the upcoming NFL Draft.
New England aims to add a third quarterback to support starter Drake Maye for the upcoming season.

title: Mass. man charged with murdering wife, allegedly told 911 operator ‘my gun went off’
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Mark Opper, 56, charged with murdering his wife, Diane, in their Belchertown home after a 911 call.
Police found Diane Opper with multiple gunshot wounds and collected evidence suggesting intentional murder.
Mark Opper is held without bail, set for a probable cause hearing on April 27 in Belchertown District Court.

title: Brockton man facing 14 charges after allegedly engaging in shootout with police
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jailson Andrade, 44, faces 14 charges after engaging in a shootout with police in Brockton, including assault and firearm charges.
Andrade was apprehended after a police chase and a shooting incident; he was struck in the shoulder during an exchange of gunfire.
A 71-year-old man was grazed by a bullet during the incident; both he and Andrade sustained non-life-threatening injuries.

title: 1 killed, 1 injured in Charlton crash, driver reportedly refused to stop for police
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A fatal crash on Route 20 in Charlton killed one driver and injured another on Tuesday morning.
A speeding pick-up truck driver refused to stop for police, causing a head-on collision with an SUV.
The injured individual was airlifted to UMass Memorial Hospital; their identities remain undisclosed.
